activities.name: FOX Kit Loans
activities.description: The awesome team from FOX are back with their kit loans. Which includes helmets and body armour. Each moto school brings their own gear too, so between your moto school and FOX, we’ve got you covered. If you have your own kit please bring it. You must follow the kit requirements specified by your riding school for the lesson you choose. Please arrive at least 15 minutes early to your moto school's meet-up point, and your instructor will guide you on what gear they’ll provide and what you might need to borrow from FOX. Wear something comfy underneath your riding gear - fitness gear such as leggings works great!

activities.name: Try A Bell Helmet
activities.description: Swing by the Bell Helmets tent and try on their incredible range of helmets. They’re not for sale, so there’s absolutely no pressure to buy - just a chance to find the right fit and see whether a Bell Helmet might be an investment worth considering in the future. The VC team won’t be seen in anything else! Don’t forget, there’s also a chance to win one of five Bell Helmets customised by some of our favourite women artists! Head over to the Camp VC Merch Stand to grab your raffle tickets.

activities.name: The Facility: VC Tattoo Studio
activities.description: We've got seven incredible artists in our very own Tattoo Studio this year, each offering exclusive "once in a lifetime" Camp VC flash. Perfect for those already inked and those considering THEIR VERY FIRST TATTOO!!! We recommend booking your slot online in advance, but there will also be a limited number of walk-up appointments available. Click here to book . If you haven't booked ahead, just swing by and see what's available!

activities.name: Calm Space
activities.description: Need a little time out? Come and relax in our Calm Space – a quiet area away from the energy of the main event site where you can take a breather, pop in some ear plugs and recharge for a while. You'll also find our friendly Access & Inclusivity Team here if you have any questions or need support during the weekend. Free period products will be available too! If full-on partying isn't your thing, you might also want to check out the chilled evening activities in the Bad Bitch Assembly.

activities.name: Reiki, Crystal Healing, Blissweave & Rahanni (£)
activities.description: The incredible team from Balance Your Being are offering 1-1 Reiki, Crystal Healing, Blissweave & Rahanni treatments throughout the weekend. We suggest booking ahead online, but some walk-in spaces may be available - so pop over and say hi to the team! Scroll down for more info on each incredible treatment. Paid Activity Booking Instructions Click the third-party link above to book and pay for your space. Important Notes - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. - Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. - Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. Blissweave Bliss Weaving is a complete guided transformation method, a gentle personal growth modality that helps you reconnect with yourself, your inner wisdom, and the next loving step in your life. Through visioning, breathing, feeling, and simple aligned action, Bliss Weaving helps you soften what feels stuck and begin weaving more clarity, confidence, joy, and connection into your everyday world. Crystal Healing Re-align your chakras and wellbeing with a crystal prescription specific to your needs. When crystals are placed on the body or within the auric field, they help to alleviate physical, emotional, mental, and spiritual ailments by removing energy blockages or negative energy within the chakras and affected areas. Each session consists of a consultation, chakra re-alignment, and a bespoke crystal prescription. Reiki Restore your body’s life force energy through this ancient Japanese practice. Reiki is Japanese for ‘universal life energy’ and is based on the belief that this energy flows freely within each of us. When we become stressed or ill, this energy can become blocked in certain parts of the body, stopping the natural flow, which can lead to further health problems. Rahanni Rahanni, meaning ‘of one heart’, is a divine healing modality that allows us to open the heart and move through challenging times. A wonderful taster session of this gentle energy, which connects with the Angels, Archangels, and Ascended Masters of Earth, whom we individually call upon.

activities.name: Baddies Book Swap
activities.description: Bring a book by or for women that’s got you fired up, whether it’s an indie mag, a classic, a memoir, or that one weird zine that changed everything. Add it to the Baddies Book Swap shelf, write a note in it if you fancy, and swap it for something new. The shelf will be there all weekend, with quiet morning sessions for anyone who wants to browse, chat books, share recommendations, or just sit with a coffee and have a nose. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Design A Custom Moto
activities.description: We’re stoked to announce that, to celebrate Camp VC’s 10th and Royal Enfield’s 125th birthdays, we’re inviting the VC community to help design a one-of-a-kind custom Himalayan. We want to see your designs capture the Camp VC spirit of freedom and adventure. Swing by the Moto Tent to pick up an entry slip and submit your tank design, along with a name for the bike. The winning tank design will feature on the custom Himalayan, created in collaboration with Royal Enfield, and she'll be unveiled next year. You won’t win the bike, we’re afraid, but you will earn the bragging rights that come with designing it!

activities.name: Tarot Card Reading
activities.description: Swing by the tarot tent, our readers will be accepting bookings via walk-up all weekend long.

activities.name: The Pleasure Project Post Box
activities.description: The Pleasure Project has been putting the sexy into safer sex since 2004. At this year's Post Office, you're invited to send a message of kindness, sparkle, or pleasure to someone at Camp VC. Your message might big up someone's skate moves, tell someone you fancy them, or simply spread some general festival love to a stranger. When your message is ready, post it, and the gorgeous Pleasure Project team will do the rest! NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Open Skateboarding
activities.description: Come and join Shankos Skate School to hang out and ride the ramps and flat ground. This is an open skate session with no coaching provided, so you’ll need previous skateboarding experience and be confident riding independently. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. No complete beginners. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Words that Move - Drop In
activities.description: A creative, immersive writing and art workshop where your expression and voice are transformed into visual form. Use the quick prompts to uncover your thoughts and truths, then transform these into bold, visual art through typography, collage and mark-making. Leave with a one-off artwork made from your own words that you can keep, gift, or swap with someone else! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Axe Throwing
activities.description: No booking required, just rock up whenever you fancy during opening hours.

activities.name: Climbing Wall
activities.description: Our 24-foot climbing wall has four climbing lanes and a range of routes with varying difficulty levels, making it suitable for both beginners and more experienced climbers. The automatic belay system makes the activity accessible for more nervous and new climbers, so come and give it a go!

activities.name: Quad Bike - Taster Experience (£)
activities.description: In these 25-minute taster sessions, you'll master the basics of riding the quad, before tackling obstacles such as tight corners, log balances and see-saws. The focus is on skill, control and confidence rather than speed. Choose between 90cc automatic quads, which are smaller and easier to handle, or more powerful 250cc semi-automatic quads. Please note that the 90cc quads have a rider weight limit of 13 stone, while the 250cc quads have a weight limit of 25 stone. Riders must have sufficient strength and mobility in both hands and feet to safely operate the controls, including braking, acceleration and gear changes where applicable. Quad biking can be physically demanding, so participants should be comfortable gripping handlebars and have a reasonable level of core strength. Helmets with visors are provided and must be worn. They weigh aprx 1.35 kg. If you have your own off-road helmet, you're welcome to bring it. Flat, sturdy footwear such as trainers, boots, or wellies must be worn. Sandals, Crocs, and other open-toed footwear are not permitted. Participants must also wear long sleeves and full-length trousers. Riders who demonstrate the required skill and control during their taster session will be invited to book onto the more advanced off-road trail, exploring the site's beautiful and rugged terrain. Trail sessions cannot be booked in advance, as riders must first demonstrate their ability in the field. Taster Experience – £25 Off-Road Experience – £25 Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click t he partner link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open 10am, Sat 4th July. 2) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. - Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. - Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: Archery
activities.description: Come and try your hand at archery and see if you can get that bullseye! No experience needed, expert coached, and open to all levels. If you miss out on a spot in Archery, remember that we've got drop-in Axe Throwing all weekend long!

activities.name: Learn to Roller Skate
activities.description: Join expert coaches from The Skate Scholarship to learn how to get rolling (and stop!) safely on Quad Skates. Perfect for complete beginners. Skates, pads and helmets are provided. You must click here and provide your shoe size when you book. If you don't, we can't guarantee that your size will be available. If you own your own skates, feel free to bring them (but inline skates aren't permitted).

activities.name: Massage with Island Feather (£)
activities.description: Island Feather are back with an incredible range of massage services, including a 25-minute seated massage (£35) and 50-minute Massage Therapy Session (£65). For 50 minute treatments choose from Deep/Soft Tissue, Clinical Massage, or a treatment focused on specific/a combination areas - including neck, shoulders, back, legs, hands and feet. Paid Activity Booking Instructions Click here to book 25 minute sessions. Click here to book 50 minute sessions. Important Notes: - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. - Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. - Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: She Shreds Hair & Beauty (£)
activities.description: Swing by the She Shreds tent for buzz cuts, undercuts, braids, mullets, hair jewellery, and more! Whether you're making a bold move or just after some wild festival styling, then Lauren and her team will sort you out! If you're brave enough to do it, shaving the VC logo is free! We just ask for a donation to charity please. All bookings are handled on site, with a mix of walk-ins and pre-booked slots available. Just pop-in and say hi!

activities.name: Copper Foil Embossing
activities.description: Use simple tools and techniques to transform sheets of copper foil into beautifully textured designs filled with pattern, detail, and shine. Inspired by traditional metalwork and folk art, this relaxing workshop is perfect for beginners and experienced makers alike. Leave with your own embossed artwork and a new skill to add to your creative toolkit. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed.

activities.name: Flower Pressing
activities.description: Tap into your inner forest gnome and learn how to press & preserve flowers and plants. Discover the steps to press your own botanicals and how to make them into beautiful, lasting art. Leave with a mini press and a one-of-a-kind piece of botanical artwork to take home with you. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed.

activities.name: Beginner Skateboarding
activities.description: Join the team from Shanko's Skate School for a skateboarding taster session, where you'll learn all of the basics! No experience needed - boards, helmets, and pads are provided. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Beginners BMX (£)
activities.description: An introduction to riding a BMX pump track, led by 9-time BMX World Champion and Olympian Shanaze Reade and her elite coaching team. Learn the fundamentals of body and pedal position, cornering, and pumping to maintain momentum around the track. The track is set up in stages, allowing more confident riders to progress onto trickier sections as they build skills. You must be able to ride a bike confidently while standing on the pedals, but no BMX experience is required. Please arrive to the session warmed up and ready to ride. For example, a brief jog around the site and some lunges would work! Trainers only. No crocs or sandals. Helmet, elbow pads, knee pads and long fingered gloves must be worn and are available for loan. Suitable trainers only, no sandals or crocs. Riders who succesfully complete the beginners lesson will be welcome to come back and ride the track during the Open Sessions. Booking Instructions: 1) Click this link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. -“Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first.

activities.name: Intro to Mini Ramps (Skateboarding)
activities.description: Learn the basics of the mini ramp: pumping, turning and maybe even dropping in! You don’t need to be a seasoned skater for this one, but you must be able to roll comfortably on a skateboard already. No complete beginners. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Sauna & Cold Plunge (£)
activities.description: We’re absolutely stoked to bring you guys a beautiful six-person wood-fired sauna, complete with two cold plunges built into the hillside at our stunning event site. You’ll be blown away by the views! 50 minute session: £35 per person. Booking instructions 1) Click this link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. -“Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first.

activities.name: Mandala Making - Drop In
activities.description: Mandalas represent wholeness and unity and have been created cross-culturally for over 2000 years. Learn the basics to make your own mandala. With a focus on pattern development and freehand drawing, you'll create your own mandala outline to colour in and decorate. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Desert Rose - Enduro School Taster Session (£)
activities.description: Not for complete beginners. This session is for those with riding experience (not necessarily on a dirt bike) who can already operate a bike with a clutch and gears. If you are a complete beginner, then check out Desert Rose’s eBike Xperience instead! This enduro bike taster session gives riders a feel for riding these amazing bikes across the terrain at Camp VC. The Desert Rose team will run you through the core principles and exercises of riding off-road, getting on and off the bikes, body positioning, bike-handling, clutch control and optimising braking skills for loose and uneven terrain. £70 for 2 hours. Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. Please click here to be taken to the booking platform. 2) Click the third-party link above to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! Please Read: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. You must sign the waiver you'll receive by email directly from Desert Rose to participate. Riders are encouraged to bring their own kit where possible: A full face helmet, MX kit jersey and trousers, MX riding boots, riding gloves A limited amount of riding kit will be available on-site through the FOX kit loans on a first-come, first-served basis. Wear whatever you like under your kit, as long as you're comfortable (fitness gear always works well) PLEASE ARRIVE AT THE MEET POINT AT LEAST 15 MINUTES EARLY TO BE COLLECTED BY THE TEAM

activities.name: Inch Perfect - Absolute Beginner to Riding Motorcycles (£)
activities.description: This session is for complete beginners to riding motorcycles, but you must be able to ride a bicycle to participate . If you already have some riding experience (but are new to off-road), check out the Inch Perfect 'Beginner to Trials Racing' session instead! Ride out for the very first time and get to grips with overall bike control. You'll ride an electric (EM) trials bike, which means you'll get going straight away, without having to master clutch and gears! £109 for 2 hours. Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. Important! Please Read: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Start on flat ground with some simple trial skills, including body position, balance, and bike handling. Progress through the course to experience more challenging obstacle navigation and advanced bike control. £109 for 2 hours. activities.name: Climbing & Abseiling Experience (£) - Off-site
activities.description: This is a paid-for experience operated by Bearded Men Adventures. Discover the stunning local scenery and experience the thrill of rock climbing and abseiling. A shuttle to / from Camp VC is included in the £55 cost. Suitable for mixed abilities, from beginner to more experienced climbers. Participants should meet at the pick-up point in the Camp VC car park at the agreed start time. Do not head directly to the Panorama Walk destination. Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click our partner's link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. -“Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first. -Queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: Mountain Bike Pump Track: Drop-In
activities.description: Come drop in and try out our legendary mountain bike pump track this weekend at Camp VC! Mountain bikes and bike helmets are provided by the awesome Llangollen-based Drosi Bikes and their all-female team of bike mechanics. Open to those who can already ride a bicycle - all levels and abilities. NOTE: You must already be able to ride a bicycle to participate in this drop-in activity Trainers only - no crocs, heels, sandals, or open-toe shoes allowed No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: QueerSwap Pop-Up Clothing Swap
activities.description: Refresh your wardrobe with the QueerSwap pop-up clothing swap! Drop items off at the swap tent on Friday and collect your swap tokens, then swing by Saturday or Sunday to find new treasures. 1 Item = 1 token. NOTE: Please ensure all items are clean and in good condition or better. We do not accept children's clothing. Socks and undergarments must be new and in their original packaging. No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Self Love Revolution Bus
activities.description: The Self Love Revolution Bus is back, baby! Open all weekend, visit Susie , Lu's gorgeous, 32-foot, vintage American bus, and meet the incredible Self Love Revolution team. Bring a drink and have a natter! Great for solo attendees, but open to all. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: The Unshrunk Collection
activities.description: Come and meet Marva, our Menopause Coach & Formulator. Having almost died after being misdiagnosed during perimenopause, Marva set about creating her own solutions to her health condition. Sitting at her mum's kitchen table, unable to walk, she started using recipes & knowledge handed down from her ancestors in the Caribbean to create a range of natural products to ease her symptoms. The Unshrunk Collection will be available to purchase at the bus all weekend. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis

activities.name: Whimsical Wardrobe of Dreams
activities.description: Step into Lu’s Whimsical Wardrobe of Dreams and into a world of freedom, love and joy! Through whimsy, play, and dress-up, we invite you to connect with your inner child and transport yourself to a time before we all became conditioned to give too much of a fuck about what people think. Before we were told how to dress, how to behave, and how to age. Get dressed up! Be brave! Feel wild! And take all the selfies your whimsical heart desires! NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis

activities.name: ADHD: Sorry Not Sorry
activities.description: Join award-winning podcaster, Laura Reynolds, for an in-depth talk on the fear and self-loathing that can come from a late diagnosis of ADHD Get ready to shame-drop, rage-channel and better come to terms with being the last in on the punchline...The joke being, your actual life! With absolutely no mentions of superpowers, you'll come away feeling validation, shame eradication, and wanting to kick a door off its hinges!

activities.name: Intro to Motorsport
activities.description: Interested in the world of motorsport, but don't know where to start to get involved? We've got you! In this myth-busting, inspiring talk, you'll explore on-track terminology and set-up, walk through the basics of getting started through track days, and discover other ways to get involved that aren't behind the wheel. Leave feeling empowered, informed, and excited about your potential future involvement in this incredible industry. Open to all - no prior experience required.

activities.name: Desert Rose - E-bike Experience (£)
activities.description: This session is for complete beginners to riding motorcycles, but you must be able to ride a bicycle to participate . If you already have some riding experience (but are new to off-road), check out the Desert Rose 'Enduro Taster’ session instead! This eBike Xperience is an opportunity for those new to motorbike riding to get out there on two wheels! Designed to give you confidence in bike handling and control, whilst getting used to the power and braking of the bikes on the CAMP VC terrain. With an expanded fleet of newly launched eBike dirt bikes, you’ll be among the very first in the UK to try out these incredible bikes! £35 for 1 hour. Please click here to be taken to the booking platform. 2) Click the third-party link above to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. activities.name: White Water Rafting (£)
activities.description: This is a paid-for activity operated by Bearded Men Adventures at the incredible rapids at LLangollen's River Dee. A shuttle to / from Camp VC is included in the £70 cost. Participants should meet at the pick-up point in the Camp VC car park at the agreed start time. Do not head directly to the River Dee. Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click this third-party link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. “Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first. Queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: Self Love Underwear Photoshoot (£)
activities.description: All too often, society makes us self-conscious, and we can lose sight of just how amazing and miraculous our bodies really are. Lu, Sassy, and Sarah have teamed up at CAMP VC to show folks of all shapes and sizes that they are beautiful. Partnering with YOU underwear, book a private, exclusive photoshoot session on the bus and get your own personal portraits as a reminder and celebration of a weekend full of self-love. £99 per photoshoot. Get the pics and keep the underwear! Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. Please click here to be taken to the booking platform . Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Click the third-party link above to book and pay for your space. 2) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important Notes: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: Forest Bathing
activities.description: Discover the practice of Shinrin-Yoku (Forest Bathing) by slowing down and reconnecting to yourself and nature. This guided workshop encourages you to be present, take in the sights, sounds, and smells around you, and allow your body to be your guide. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Bring a yoga mat or similar to sit on Layer up and prepare for all weathers Bring water and any personal effects you may need off-site (e.g., personal medication)

activities.name: Fun Football Skills
activities.description: Never kicked a ball? Haven't kicked a ball in a while? We've got you! Come join us to learn some new skills and build your confidence with the ball. Get stuck in with some fun warm-ups and basic ball skills, and discover key tips and techniques. Put your newfound tekkers into practice with some simple group games and challenges. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Enter CAMP VC's weekend-long keepy-uppy challenge for a chance to win a prize during Sunday's football tournament!

activities.name: Nettle Cordage Making
activities.description: Learn to make nettle cordage by harvesting and processing nettles to extract their fibres and braid them to make natural string. You can then make your own nettle bracelet or necklace! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Wild & Cyclical: Adventure that works with your cycle
activities.description: A workshop for adventurous people who menstruate who want to reclaim their rhythm in a world built around the male 24‑hour cycle. You’ll learn to use your menstrual cycle as a map — a guide for movement, adventure, and resilience. Through menstrual cycle awareness, you’ll understand how each phase shapes your energy and recovery, helping you explore with more strength, confidence, and connection to yourself.

activities.name: Bicycle Puncture Repair
activities.description: The dreaded puncture! It can happen at any time, and if (or when!) it does, what are you going to do? Having the confidence to fix a puncture is a game-changer for any cyclist. Drosi bikes will cover everything you’ll need to tackle puncture issues. Learn how to remove wheels and tyres, repair or replace tubes, and get the full rundown on the equipment you need. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Knitting - Drop in
activities.description: Discover the timeless art of knitting! Practice the knit stitch, increases and decreases, and how to cast off a tiny project to take home with you. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Folks who want to refresh their knitting skills are very welcome to join, but it's unlikely we'll have the capacity to troubleshoot on your specific project No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Lino cut: Playful Landscapes
activities.description: Create playful, simple linocut landscapes inspired by nature. Learn how to transfer your design and master techniques for lino carving and printing. Use your own reference photos, imagine your own landscape, or take inspiration from our beautiful surroundings in the lush Welsh mountains. You'll leave with an A5 print and a new artistic skill that you can easily continue at home! Perfect for beginners, but open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed

activities.name: Days on the Dirt - Beginner Riding Class (£)
activities.description: This session is for complete beginners to riding motorcycles, but you must be able to ride a bicycle to participate . (If you already have some riding experience, check out the other moto school classes for non-beginners.) Get your very first taste of motorcycle riding on a 125cc Honda CRF mini dirt bike! In this workshop designed for total beginners, you’ll learn the very basics of riding, from gears, bike controls and bike handling. The Days on the Dirt team will have you burning around on your 125 in no time, and you’ll leave feeling capable of riding a motorcycle. £65 for 1.5 hours (plus £2 booking fee) Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. Please click here to be taken to the booking platform. 2) Click the third-party link above to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! Please Read: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. You must sign the waiver you'll receive by email directly from Inch Perfect to participate. Days on the Dirt provide bikes, insurance, helmets, boots, pants and jerseys in a broad range of sizes. A limited amount of riding kit will be available on-site through the FOX kit loans on a first-come, first-served basis. Wear whatever you like under the kit, as long as you're comfortable (fitness gear always works well) PLEASE ARRIVE AT THE MEETING POINT AT LEAST 15 MINUTES BEFORE YOUR START TIME

activities.name: Lantern Making
activities.description: Light up Camp VC with the magic of lantern making. Using willow, tissue paper, and a little imagination, you’ll build your own beautiful illuminated sculpture from the ground up. Whether inspired by nature, folklore, or your own creative vision, every lantern becomes a unique work of art. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Please bring your own torch or head torch to light up your lantern for the lantern display.

activities.name: QueerSwap Textile Repair Station
activities.description: Need a quick patch or want to add some flair to an item of clothing? Check out the QueerSwap repair and embroidery station, complete with tools to make simple textile repairs and instruction guides for visible mending and embroidery. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Quad Bike - Off Road Trail (£)
activities.description: Riders who demonstrate the required skill and control during a quad bike taster session will be invited to book onto the more advanced off-road trail, exploring the site's beautiful and rugged terrain. Trail sessions cannot be booked in advance, as riders must first demonstrate their ability in the field. Bookings will be made on site, with cash and card payments accepted. For your chance to ride the trail, get booked into a quad bike taster experience first!

activities.name: Basic Car Mechanics
activities.description: Are you interested in car mechanics and in giving your own car some much-deserved TLC, but don't know where to start? Discover all the need-to-know basics with practical demonstrations, maintenance checks, car safety insights, and pre-road trip checks from our expert mechanic. Come to this safe and inclusive space armed with any questions on mechanics, and leave feeling confident and inspired! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Hips Don't Lie
activities.description: A somatic movement workshop aimed at releasing tension stored in the hips, navigating breath, mindful awareness, and intuitive dance flow using sophrology. Sophrology blends techniques from yoga, Zen Buddhism, progressive muscle relaxation, and other practices to offer a uniquely versatile approach to embodiment for everyday life. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: How to Unshrink Yourself
activities.description: Join us to explore how to un-shrink yourself, with Lu’s top tips for cultivating self-love. In this funny and heartfelt talk based on Lu's personal journey from a wife and mum to an accidental 50+ influencer, together we'll cover everything from divorce and menopause to motherhood and grief, plus a whole lot more! Be prepared to leave feeling un-shrunk, confident, and recklessly optimistic!

activities.name: Intro to Campervans
activities.description: Dreaming about your first campervan, planning a self-build, or wanting to get more from your current setup? Explore the key considerations for choosing the right van for your budget, discover different campervan layouts, and get expert insights into what it takes to build a campervan from the ground up. Suitable for those just starting, those halfway through a build, or seasoned campervanners with experience to share!

activities.name: Logo and Livery Design
activities.description: Discover the world of automotive graphic design and see how logo and livery graphics can transform the identity, performance perception, and personality of a vehicle. Consider proportion, flow, brand storytelling, and how graphics wrap around complex forms as you sketch your very own livery design. Once done, you'll translate your design into vinyl and apply it to a model vehicle to gain practical insight into how two-dimensional graphics adapt to three-dimensional surfaces. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Remote Control Car Racing - Drop In
activities.description: Enjoyed all the big action at CAMP VC and now fancy some small action? Show off your technical handling skills with remote-controlled racing cars on our Crawler Obstacle Course! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Atlas Stones
activities.description: Get hands-on with the iconic atlas stones and learn proper lifting technique for safe, powerful lifting. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Deadlift
activities.description: Build full-body strength and technique as you learn how to lift heavy weights from the ground safely and efficiently. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Dumbell Press
activities.description: Learn the single-arm dumbbell clean and press, a staple event in StrongWoman for building shoulder strength and balance. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Moto Talk: Trip Planning
activities.description: How to plan your next trip, on the roads you actually want to ride. Whether going for a couple of days or several weeks, moto travelling requires some solid planning to make the most of your trip. From packing to route planning, your next adventure starts here!

activities.name: Signwriting
activities.description: Learn how to custom paint using traditional sign-writing hand-painting techniques, take home what you create, and use your new skill set to paint up your own things! Also, make sure you swing by the VC moto tent where Jorge will be custom sign painting your personal helmets, skateboards, or skate helmets with your name for £30 a pop all weekend long!

activities.name: Welcome Yoga
activities.description: Begin the weekend with a sequence of connected yoga poses. Focus on flow, breath, and balance through surya namaskar (sun salutations) and creative āsana (physical postures). With some strongholds, as well as time to rest, you'll have options to fire it up and slow it down depending on how you feel. NOTE: Open to all levels and abilities Mats are provided Wear something comfortable for movement If any yoga session is fully booked but you brought your own mat to CAMP VC, then feel free to join in!

activities.name: Strong Woman Grip Competition
activities.description: Just how grippy is YOUR grip? The epic Strong Woman crew is back this year with their legendary Strong Woman Grip competition. See if you have what it takes to be CAMP VC's 2026 winner by clocking the longest grip time on the leaderboard! Open to all levels and abilities - No experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Ask Aunty ADHD
activities.description: What's the Most ADHD thing you’ve done this week? Laura Reynolds, award-winning podcaster, will be at the bus all weekend long celebrating neurodiversity, interviewing people for her podcast, and answering any questions you may have about ADHD. Think lemonade-stand-meets-podcast! NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis

activities.name: Advanced Ramp Tricks (Skateboarding)
activities.description: One for the more advanced skaters out there. Come join the incredible team from Shankos Skate School and learn some tricks on the coping! No complete beginners. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed.

activities.name: Football Skills & Drills
activities.description: Want to test your footie skills and level up your tekkers? Learn new skills to use in a variety of common game scenarios and perfect them through fun drills and group challenges. Think: golden goal moments, defence drills and fancy footwork. Just what you need to look epic on the field! Perfect for those with some footy playing experience, but open to all. NOTE: Enter CAMP VC's weekend-long keepy-uppy challenge for a chance to win a prize during Sunday's football tournament!

activities.name: Get into Climbing
activities.description: Never climbed before, but curious to find out more? We've got you. In this fun, games-based talk, we'll show you how to safely put on a harness and how to belay for the first time. All done from the ground with no actual climbing in the session, this is the perfect introduction to build your climbing confidence for the future. Perfect for beginners with no prior climbing experience. NOTE: No climbing takes place in this session Trousers or long short recommended

activities.name: Outdoor Cooking
activities.description: Learn how to make and sustain a fire for cooking, explore how to set up a simple and efficient prep and cooking station. You'll then be treated to an expert cooking demonstration and tasting of a simple but delicious vegan recipe. You'll leave inspired and ready to elevate your next wild cookout! NOTE: The demo recipe may vary from session to session, but it will always be suitable for vegans It is not guaranteed that the demo recipes will be gluten or nut-free. If you have any dietary allergies, you must declare them to the workshop host before the beginning of the session

activities.name: Patch Painting - Drop in
activities.description: Explore how to create unique patches that will have you levelling up your wardrobe the minute you get home! Learn the techniques and tools to design, draw, and paint onto a denim patch. Unleash your creativity with your own design or take inspiration from our pre-drawn designs. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Cocktail Making with Jimmy's
activities.description: Come and join the Jimmy’s crew as we serve up a coffee cocktail workshop to get the party started! We’ll be mixing up 3 coffee-based cocktails, keeping things light, fun and hands-on (non-alcoholic option and non milk based option available too). Come and have a laugh, and learn a few new skills. Leave with a goodie bag and possibly a new favourite way to drink your Jimmy’s.

activities.name: Watercolour Tattoo Flash
activities.description: Always wondered how tattoo artists create flash sheets with blended watercolour? Now is your chance to learn! You’ll be guided through watercolour techniques to create your own tattoo-inspired paintings to take home and frame. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed

activities.name: Alter Ego
activities.description: A seated confidence-building workshop from BodiBlossom, exploring the idea of the alter ego. Through writing, reflection and guided prompts, you’ll create a version of yourself that can help you feel bolder, braver and more able to take up space. This isn't about pretending to be someone else. More about finding the bits of you that are already there, and giving them a name, a shape, and a bit more room.

activities.name: Basic Power Tools & Carpentry
activities.description: Join us for this practical introduction to carpentry and power tool use. Discover the essentials: how to use a drill safely, identifiers for different types of wall plugs, and the best methods for drilling holes. You'll also get hands-on with a jigsaw and practice how to cut out wooden shapes. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Campervan Electrics
activities.description: Join us as we de-mystify the world of campervan electrics, make sense of the numbers, and understand how all the pieces fit together. Get hands-on with the key components of a campervan electrical system, including battery technologies, solar power, inverters, fuses, circuit protectors, and much more. Hear real-world examples and advice on selecting the right components for your build, and yes— dabble in a little maths! Suitable for those just starting, those halfway through a build, or seasoned campervanners with experience to share!

activities.name: Let's Talk Menopause Wellness
activities.description: Menopause Wellness aims to help those who experience menopause navigate it with confidence, knowledge, and hope. Hear Marva's own story and ask anything in this safe and welcoming space. Following a near-death experience in 2019 that left her fighting for her health and mobility, Dr. (h.c) Marva Williams turned adversity into purpose, and is now an award-winning Menopause Coach, Counsellor, author, and founder of Shhh… Menopause Wellness

activities.name: Wheels, Brakes and Suspension
activities.description: Ever wanted to know if something's wrong with your car before your mechanic tells you? Get stuck in and learn how to change a wheel, what to look out for with brakes and suspension, fault-finding processes, insights on driving systems and all the need-to-knows to keep your car running smoothly. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Acrobalance
activities.description: Acrobalance is a dynamic circus discipline where pairs, trios, and groups work together to create striking acrobatic shapes and impressive formations. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Axle Overhead
activities.description: Master pressing the thick-handled axle bar overhead with power and control, developing stability and coordination. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Farmer's Carry
activities.description: Test your grip and total-body endurance by walking with heavy implements in each hand, focusing on posture and control. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: General Bicycle Maintenance
activities.description: Maintain your bike, and your bike will maintain you! Join Drosi Bikes for this general bicycle maintenance workshop. Learn how to perform an ‘M check’ (safety check) and discover key tips on how to keep your bike happy and working smoothly. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Intermediate Skateboarding Tricks
activities.description: If you can already ollie while rolling, this class is for you. Join us to learn pop and flip tricks. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Hiking for Beginners
activities.description: The mountains don’t have to be intimidating. In this invaluble talk, we’ll break down hiking into the basics such as what to wear, what to pack, how to prepare for changing weather, and how to approach a day in the hills with confidence. Expect practical guidance, real-world examples, personal stories, and lots of opportunities to ask questions. Perfect for beginners, but open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Felt Pin Badge Making - Drop In
activities.description: Create your own felt pin badge using simple stitching technique, colourful beads, and a safety pin to finish. Explore the use of shapes, colours, and textures to turn felt into a small-but-mighty statement piece that you'll keep as a CAMP VC souvenir! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Headline Moto Talk: Patsy Quick
activities.description: Patsy Quick is a pioneering British rally rider who made history in 2006 as the first British woman to complete the Dakar Rally, achieving her goal after overcoming serious injury and multiple previous attempts. Since then, she has successfully channelled her Dakar experience into business, founding Desert Rose Racing and the Desert Rose Adventure Riding Academy, where she trains, mentors, and supports riders from beginner level through to international rally competition. Grab a drink and come hear how she's done it all, and what's next. Her Dakar achievements and subsequent work as a coach, team manager and entrepreneur have made her one of the most influential figures in British adventure motorcycling, and she's here to talk through the good, bad and ugly, and tell us what's next.

activities.name: Foraging
activities.description: Join us for CAMP VC's guided foraging walk to learn how to safely and sustainably identify and gather wild food. You'll discover nutritious and medicinal plants and fungi that grow all around us, be guided through identification processes, learn about preparation methods and wild food recipes, plus hear some connected folklore! Leave the 90min adventure feeling empowered and confident for your future foraging journey. NOTE: Wear appropriate footwear Bring water and any personal effects needed for being off-site (e.g personal medication) Prepare for all weathers Get to the Adventure meet point before the start time to ensure a prompt start

activities.name: Ramp Quad Skating - Open Session
activities.description: If you have some transition experience and can already pump on a ramp, then this one's for you! Epic coaches from The Skate Scholarship will be on hand to help both beginner ramp skaters and more experienced skaters take their skills to the next level. Just turn up any time during the two hour session. As the ramp sesions are not suitable for complete beginners, you'll need to bring your own skates. Helmets and pads will be available to borrow. No in-line skates.

activities.name: VC Choir
activities.description: Join a fun, uplifting choir session led by a Llangollen local choir leader and discover the joy of singing together. Expect contemporary mash-ups, plenty of energy and a welcoming atmosphere where everyone can get involved. No singing experience is required, and there'll be no pressure to sing solo. Whether you're a seasoned performer or someone who only sings in the shower, this is all about having fun, building confidence and making some noise together. Note that the location isn't a mistake: this session will be held in our Quad Skate tent.

activities.name: Fire Building
activities.description: Come channel your inner fire-starter and learn how to build a fire using primitive fire lighting techniques and natural resources. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Wood Whittling
activities.description: Learn how to hone your knife skills for whittling wood and whittle your own little sculpture to take away as a CAMP VC souvenir! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Financial Foreplay
activities.description: Ever felt like money talk creates challenges in your relationship? Join the fabulous Sarah Maxwell to discover how to talk about money with a partner and how it can revolutionise your connection, shift any shame, and help you to successfully work together on the topic of finance to create the life you choose. Open to all - both individuals and couples welcome.

activities.name: Remote Control Car Racing
activities.description: Enjoyed all the big action at CAMP VC and now fancy some small action? Show off your technical handling skills with remote-controlled racing cars on our Crawler Obstacle Course! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name:
The Pleasure Playground:
Shame Shifting
activities.description: Ready to unlock shame around your body and sexuality? Join sex coach and pleasure activist Jodie McLean to reclaim your sensuality, power, and purpose. Expect somatic practices, embodiment, and raw honesty to help shift shame and awaken self-love. There'll be laughter, release, and the aim for you to leave with a fresh connection to your sensual self.

activities.name: Drag King Workshop
activities.description: Be your own prince! Ever wanted to try drag, but not sure where to start? Discover the basics of masculine drag performance, including: - Character and persona - Make up and facial hair - Costume, packing, and binding - Performing No experience required. All genders welcome. Fiercely trans inclusive.

activities.name: Aqueduct Canoeing (£)
activities.description: This is a paid-for activity operated by Bearded Men Adventures, offering a stunning paddle across the Pontcysyllte Aqueduct – the highest aqueduct in the world! A shuttle to / from Camp VC is included in the £60 cost. All participants should meet at the pick-up point in the Camp VC car park at the agreed start time. Do not head directly to the aqueduct. Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click this third-party link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. “Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first. Queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name:
Burlesque 
Fans
activities.description: Explore the elegance and allure of classic burlesque in this beginner-friendly fan dance workshop, where you'll learn the sensual secrets of tease, timing, and tantalising movement with small and large fans. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Tank Tattoos
activities.description: Create your very own vinyl decal tattoos! Design and paint your own custom decal to take away - ready to tattoo it onto your motorcycle tank, skateboard, helmet, water bottle, or wherever you fancy! Take inspiration from a pre-designed tattoo flash or create your own freehand piece. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Merit Patch Making
activities.description: Get ready to stitch your story at Camp VC. This merit patch workshop is all about celebrating effort, new skills, and the moments that matter. Create your own custom patch, inspired by a challenge you’ve faced, something you’ve learned, or a win worth wearing. Leave with a handmade patch and a beautiful, visual reminder of your story. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed

activities.name: Outdoor Route Planning & Navigation
activities.description: Learn the essentials of planning and navigating your own outdoor adventures with total confidence. This hands-on, introductory-level workshop covers everything from key aspects of map reading and understanding different terrains to choosing and designing new adventure routes. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Hike
activities.description: Join us for CAMP VC's laid-back, guided hike through the stunning Welsh hills. Led by experienced outdoor guides who’ll help you build confidence on the hiking trail. Whether you’re new to hiking or a seasoned pro, this is your chance to explore the local area, meet new people, and soak up the stunning views. Open to all levels and abilities, this is a social event, and the group sets the pace. No hiker is left behind, and there will be plenty of opportunities to enjoy the views, take photos, eat snacks, and chat along the way. NOTE: Wear appropriate footwear Bring water and optional snacks Bring any personal effects you would normally take on a run (e.g., personal medication) Prepare for all weathers Get to the Adventure meet point before the start time to ensure a prompt start

activities.name: Big Feeling Club (£)
activities.description: During this two-hour circle, we’ll celebrate our achievements, move through stuck emotions, make some noise, shake off what we’re carrying, and reconnect with ourselves through somatic and healing practices. Our smaller group size allows for a more nurturing and connected experience than the larger festival workshops, ensuring every participant feels seen, supported, and able to engage at a level that feels comfortable for them. Expect laughter, maybe a few tears, plenty of support, and a goody bag worth over £20, leaving you feeling truly looked after. Spaces are intentionally limited to maintain the intimacy and quality of the experience. We ask that you show up as you are and leave as yourself. Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click t his link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open 10 am, Sat 4th July. 2) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. - Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. - Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. About your hosts Leading the workshop are Bec and Raichelle. Raichelle is a horse-mad Reiki and Blissweave practitioner who is passionate about bringing calm, relaxation and bliss to her healing work. With a keen interest in nature and the natural world, she weaves these elements into the nurturing support she provides through her sessions. Bec is a neurospicy anime geek and, through Balance Your Being, teaches Reiki, Crystal Healing and Rahanni, as well as delivering workshops on stress, hormones, meditation and coaching. She is a passionate advocate for mental and menstrual health and, alongside Raichelle, has facilitated healing circles, women's circles, menstrual health support circles, Reiki shares, wellbeing workshops and one-to-one sessions. Together, they create safe nooks for people to show up, connect with themselves, and process emotions in a healthy and empowering way.

activities.name: Lego-tation - Drop In
activities.description: What do you get when you combine LEGO and meditation? Lego-tation! Come chill with us at the Auto Tent for our laid-back Lego-tation sessions. Grab a drink, build a LEGO car, and connect with other awesome attendees while you're at it. Stay for one build or keep going. It’s all at your pace. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: BMX Open Track
activities.description: Anyone who successfully completes a BMX lesson at CAMP - or can demonstrate previous BMX experience to the coaches - is welcome to use the pump track during the open track sessions. Just turn up anytime during opening hours! Bikes, helmets, and protective kit will be available to borrow. This session is not suitable for complete beginners. Coaches will be supervising the track, but this is not a coached session. Riders must be able to ride safely and independently; anyone unable to do so may be asked to leave the track. Trainers only please - no Crocs or sandals.

activities.name: Basic Moto Mechanics
activities.description: Master the basics of motorbike care with our ever-popular Basic Moto Mechanics workshop. Designed to empower you with essential moto skills, including regular motorcycle checks, repairing a tubeless tyre, and doing your own chain maintenance. Perfect for riders but open to all - no experience required.

activities.name: Nipple Tassel Twirling
activities.description: Learn the fabulous burlesque art of tassel twirling. Get ready to throw out self-doubt and bloom into body confidence, self-love, and power! Tassels are provided and are yours to keep after the session! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Reggaeton Dance Party
activities.description: A fun, high-energy dance session with a reggaeton style and flavour. Follow simple, repeated movements for each song and dance at your own pace. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. Wear comfortable clothing and footwear suitable for dancing in.

activities.name: DIY Silkscreen Printing
activities.description: In this interactive demo, discover how to create amazing DIY silk screen printing at home, without all the expensive equipment. Walk through the step-by-step process in an immersive tutorial, which culminates in the chance to have a go yourself on one of our pre-made, DIY screens. You'll leave with your very own DIY silkscreen print to take home. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Advanced Skateboard Jam
activities.description: Join us for tunes, tricks, mini competitions, spot prizes, hippy jump challenges, and more. Whether you're riding or watching, come get involved! This jam isn't suited to complete beginners, but beginners are welcome to join our beginner friendly jam on Saturday night. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Solo Drinks Social
activities.description: It's the legendary Friday Solo Social! Coming to CAMP VC on your own? We've got you! Grab a drink and then swing by the BBA tent for a chilled hang with other badass solo attendees. No awkward intros, just good people and even gooder times! NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Friday Roller Disco
activities.description: D-I-S-C-O! Join us for the Camp VC Roller Disco! Open to quad skaters with existing experience, or anyone who has successfully completed a Beginners Lesson and mastered the basics. Skates, helmets and pads are available to borrow on a first-come, first-served basis, although we strongly encourage you to bring your own equipment. No inline skates or first-time skaters, please. Helmets and pads are mandatory. Drop in at any point during the 90-minute session. If it's busy, participants may be asked to take turns so everyone gets a chance to skate.

activities.name: Reggaeton Routine
activities.description: Learn a fun choreographed reggaeton routine to a booty-shaking soundtrack. Structured to build confidence, rhythm, and performance skills in the reggaeton dance style. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. Wear comfortable clothing and footwear suitable for dancing in.

activities.name: Life Drawing & Drinks
activities.description: Get inspired by the human form in this relaxed, boozy life drawing session! Bring your favourite tipple and settle in for an evening of creativity, laughter, and expressive mark-making. We'll start with a series of short, playful drawing exercises designed to loosen you up (and not just artistically!), before exploring simple techniques for measuring proportion and drawing from observation. You'll then put everything together in a longer pose, creating your own expressive figure drawing in a fun, informal atmosphere. BYOB encouraged! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: As One: Ensemble Workshop
activities.description: Join us to explore connection, community, and creating ensemble works through group movement and theatre devising techniques. No previous experience necessary, open to all levels and abilities.

activities.name: Founders Talk & Team Talk
activities.description: Join us at the Main Stage as Lu Featherstone joins VC founder Gemma Harrison to reflect on ten years of Camp VC, sharing the stories, lessons and moments that have shaped the community from an East London car park to the festival we know today. We’re also delighted to welcome Shauna Coxsey, Britain’s most decorated competitive climber, for a conversation on ambition, motherhood, the evolution of climbing, and what the future holds for women in the sport.

activities.name: Lap Dancing
activities.description: New for 2026, join us for this epic, confidence-boosting Magic Mike -style lap dance workshop, brought to you by the incredible Bodiblossom. We’ll use chairs to explore playful movement, performance energy, masc and femme roles and self-expression in a safe, supportive and encouraging space. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Wear comfortable clothing you can move in Trainers recommended. No heels, please. Some elements of this workshop involve non-contact partner work, but you don't need to bring someone with you to take part!

activities.name: Listening Party
activities.description: Beats on, vibes high. These drop-in sessions are a space to just sit, chat, and listen to some tunes. Playlists will range from spacey techno to ambient downtime. Check out the front of the BBA tent for what's playing and come hang with us if the vibe sounds good!

activities.name: Camp Fire & Marshmallows
activities.description: Bring your drink of choice and join us to cosy up by the Camp VC campfire and toast some marshmallows. Marshmallows are on the house, and vegan options are available. No need to book-in, just drop-by!

activities.name: Disco Bingo
activities.description: Disco Bingo is back for 2026!!! Expect pop hit-filled madness, epic prizes and (of course) a few surprises. Last year’s Disco Bingo featured a cardboard cut-out of Pedro Pascal and a collection of air-bed pumps, so your guess is as good as ours as to your hosts, The Dance Sissies, have in store this year! No booking needed—just show up! Spaces are allocated on a first-come first-served basis.

activities.name: Quiet Social Zone - ND Friendly
activities.description: Need a little break from the evening entertainment or generally feeling a bit overwhelmed? Drop in to the BBA tent for some calm beats and play some cards, read a book, do some light crafts, or have a little chat. Our quiet, ND-friendly social sessions provide a moment of collective chill amongst the ever-exciting activity at CAMP VC. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Karaoke
activities.description: Whether your go-to karaoke song is Cher or RATM, we invite you to get those vocal cords ready to sing it loud and proud at our Friday night Karaoke get-down.

activities.name: DJ Requests
activities.description: What are you in the mood for? WAP? RATM? On Friday night, you're the DJ!

activities.name: Sunrise Aqueduct Canoeing (£)
activities.description: This is a paid-for activity operated by Bearded Men Adventures, offering a stunning sunrise paddle across the Pontcysyllte Aqueduct – the highest aqueduct in the world! A shuttle to / from Camp VC is included in the £60 cost. All participants should meet at the pick-up point in the Camp VC car park at the agreed start time. Do not head directly to the aqueduct. Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click this third-party link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. “Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first. Queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: Morning Meditation
activities.description: A guided mindful practice that offers present-moment awareness and peace of mind to start the day. NOTE: Feel free to bring a cushion, yoga block, or rolled-up jumper to sit on, and a layer to stay warm Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required

activities.name: Vinyasa Yoga
activities.description: Mixing contemporary and traditional yogic principles, vinyasa yoga links movement to breath. With a focus on flow, we'll move fluidly through surya namaskar (sun salutations) and creative āsana (physical postures). NOTE: Open to all levels and abilities Mats are provided Wear something comfortable for movement If any yoga session is fully booked but you brought your own mat to CAMP VC, then feel free to join in!

activities.name: Buddy Up for a Self-Led Ride Out!
activities.description: Bought your motorbike to CAMP VC this weekend? Swing by the Moto Tent and check out our suggested routes for self-led ride outs on the Moto notice boards. Whilst ride-outs to these routes won't be guided, if you're planning one and want to buddy up with other riders for the trip, then meet at the Moto Tent at 9 am Saturday to assemble your crew! NOTE: We recommend that riders choose routes that match their riding experience level and confidence. These routes are unguided, and you are fully responsible for your own safety. Prefer a guided ride out? Check out the Guided Rides with Royal Enfield. Be warned, though - spaces on those guided rides will be snapped up FAST!

activities.name: Trail Run
activities.description: Join us for CAMP VC's relaxed, party-paced 6(ish) km trail run. We start our trail on-site, then progress to some beautiful local woodland trails. Open to all levels and abilities, this is a social event, and the group sets the pace. No runner is left behind, and there will be plenty of opportunities to enjoy the views, take photos, eat snacks, and chat along the way. NOTE: Please make sure you wear appropriate footwear Bring water and optional snacks Bring any personal effects you would normally take on a run (e.g personal medication) Prepare for all weathers Get to the Adventure meet point before the start time to ensure a prompt start

activities.name: Quiet Riot: Coffee & Book Club
activities.description: A morning drop-in session for early risers, solo wanderers, or anyone who wants somewhere to sit and chill before the day properly kicks off. Grab a coffee from the café, come browse the Baddies Book Swap, read for a bit, add to the postbox, find a friendly face, or just sit quietly in the tent and get yourself ready for the action ahead.

activities.name: Gong Bath
activities.description: Gong Baths are an ancient form of meditative practice that can help you find a deeper state of relaxation by reducing the "noise" in your brain. Get comfortable and bathe in the gong's surprisingly powerful vibrations. NOTE: Wear comfortable, warm clothing Bring a yoga mat to lie on (we have some to borrow on a first-come first served basis) Bring a blanket and/or pillow if you want to be extra cosy IMPORTANT: If you have tinnitus, epilepsy or are pregnant, we advise you not to participate in the Going Bath. Whilst most people feel relaxed, fall asleep or see vivid colours during the experience, some may experience a level of anxiety or discomfort. Sometimes just opening your eyes and breathing can help settle this, but please don't hesitate to get up and leave the session if you need to, or find support from one of our volunteers.

activities.name: Quad Skate - Open Session
activities.description: This chilled session gives quad skaters with existing experience, or those who have mastered the basics in a Beginners Lesson, the chance to freestyle on the flat ground. Skates, helmets and pads are available to borrow on a first-come, first-served basis, although we strongly encourage you to bring your own equipment. No inline skates or first-time skaters, please. There’s no formal coaching, but coaches from The Skate Scholarship will be on hand to offer tips and guidance. If it's busy, participants may be asked to take turns so everyone gets a chance to skate.

activities.name: Royal Enfield - Guided Ride Out
activities.description: You asked for guided ride-outs; we listened, and Royal Enfield are helping make it happen! To celebrate Camp VC’s 10th birthday and Royal Enfield’s 125th, we’ll be taking small groups out on Enfields, on a ride-out through sweeping mountain roads and rolling valleys. You'll experience one of Wales’ most breathtaking routes, winding through the foothills of the Berwyn Mountains and up to the iconic Horseshoe Pass. Please meet at the Royal Enfield Ride-Out Meeting Tent (just past reception) at the start time. The session is 90 mins long total, which includes a briefing and around 60 mins of riding time. Places are limited, so if you're a licenced rider, don't miss out! NOTE: You must hold a full licence to take part You will be given a Royal Enfield bike for the ride out - do not bring your own motorbike to the activity You will need to bring your own helmet and protective moto clothing You can book your space here in the app, and a Royal Enfield sign-up form will also be emailed to you.
