activities.name: Rock Climbing & Belay Building
activities.description: Know your rock climbing and belay basics, but keen to advance your technique? Then this talk is a bit of you! Learn how to make improvements to your rope skills, create safe belays with 2 or 3 anchors, and how to belay in new and different ways. All done from the ground with no actual climbing in the session, this is the perfect way to level up your current climbing skills ahead of a session on the VC climbing wall or your local centre! Perfect for those with some prior climbing experience, but open to all levels and abilities. NOTE: No climbing takes place in this session Trousers or long short recommended

activities.name: Tote Bag Embroidery - Drop In
activities.description: Learn the basics of hand embroidery to create your CAMP VC customised tote bag. Follow the step-by-step process of embroidering the CAMP VC logo onto a tote bag, which you'll then keep as a lovely souvenir! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Curious Coco Vintage Designs
activities.description: Curious Coco creates beautiful, one-of-a-kind items and vintage pieces at their small studio in the heart of Digbeth, Birmingham’s creative hub. Made with love just for you - come and purchase unique, reworked pre-loved garments, sparkly sequinned kimonos, or capes.

activities.name: Intro to Reiki
activities.description: Relax and recharge as you prepare for the weekend with this Reiki Taster Workshop. Join Bec and Raichelle from the Balance Your Being Tent as they introduce the principles of Reiki and explore how energy healing can support wellbeing. The session will begin with a gentle exercise to help you tune into your own energy, followed by a relaxing group Reiki experience.

activities.name: Cyanotype Prints
activities.description: Cyanotype is an early photographic printing process that uses sunlight to create striking blue-and-white prints. Using naturally found objects gathered around the CAMP VC site, we’ll make unique sun prints inspired by the landscape around us. Bring your curiosity and enjoy a slow creative moment, resulting in your own artwork to take away. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Lino cut: Stamps
activities.description: Design, carve, and print your own linocut, stamp-inspired prints. After creating a stamp design, you'll learn how to transfer it onto lino and master the techniques for carving and printing. Create your own design or use our reference images if you’re seeking inspiration. Leave with an A5 print and a new artistic skill that you can easily continue at home! Perfect for beginners, but open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed.

activities.name: Moto Talk: Riding in Europe
activities.description: Discover just how easy it is to explore Europe on a motobike! Get the full rundown from our expert riders on all the need-to-knows before you set off on your epic Euro adventure, along with tips and tricks for planning, packing, routes, destination recommendations, and much more!

activities.name: Photography with Amy Shore
activities.description: See Camp VC through a new lens with this immersive photography workshop. Start with a relaxed introduction to composition, light, and storytelling before heading out to explore the festival and capture images that catch your eye. Also learn simple but powerful editing techniques using Snapseed to transform photos and develop personal style. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Download the Snapseed app before the session start time Bring a phone you can take photos on (or a camera that connects to your phone) Part of this session moves around the site, so please wear appropriate footwear and clothing

activities.name: The Bad Bitch Manifesto
activities.description: A cut-and-paste, creative collage workshop to help you think about who you are, what matters to you, what you’re done with, and how you want to show up. Using prompts, stickers, words and collage, you’ll build your own Bad Bitch Manifesto. Being a bad bitch isn’t about being loud, performative or pretending to have it all sorted. It’s about being more connected to yourself and less swayed by everyone else’s noise. You’ll leave with something visual, personal and yours to take away.

activities.name: Freedom in Restraint: Rope and Kink Talk
activities.description: A talk and a workshop all tangled together! Join us to discover the entry into rope play and shibari, with some beginner-level knots and ties to explore on your own (or with others!) Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Life Drawing
activities.description: Get inspired by the human form in this relaxing guided drawing workshop. You will loosen up your creativity with short and sweet exercises before learning how to measure proportion and draw from observation. Finally, combining all the techniques you have learned, you will study one longer pose to create an expressive figure drawing. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Mosaic Magic
activities.description: Dive into the colourful art of mosaics! Discover the step-by-step process of turning tiles, wood, and grout into a beautiful, lasting piece of art. You'll leave with a new set of creative skills and a lovely mosaic of your own making. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed.

activities.name: Moto-Handling: How To Pick Up a Bike with Royal Enfield
activities.description: Does getting your bike in and out of a tight spot still give you the chills? Do you struggle with centre stands or worry about turning your bike round? Join us for a confidence-building, practical session on manual bike handling with Royal Enfield. Discover that managing a heavy bike isn't about your strength; it’s all about your technique. Learn about balance points, leverage and how to position yourself during manoeuvring. Hear tips on how to wrangle a big bike when you're not actually riding it. Perfect for those who already ride a motorbike, but open to all who are interested.

activities.name: Charcoal Making
activities.description: Join us for a hands-on workshop where we’ll turn willow into beautiful artist’s charcoal using fire and simple traditional techniques. Learn how to prepare the willow, make charcoal from it in the flames, then use it to draw and mark-make. You'll also leave with your own handmade charcoal to continue experimenting with at home! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Knot Tying
activities.description: Learn how to tie different knots and explore their specific uses and where best to use them. Then put your new knot-tying skills to the test by setting up an outdoor working area that keeps your food supplies off the ground! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Welsh Language Taster Class
activities.description: Wyt ti eisiau dysgu Cymraeg? We're so proud to host Camp VC in our beloved Wales each year, so what better way to get the most out of your trip than to learn some Welsh yourself? Join Elen Clampitt for an amazing workshop where you'll learn the basics of speaking Welsh and leave with a few fun phrases under your belt. Perfect for beginners, but open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: This activity will be held at different locations during the weekend, so please double-check the exact location for your specific booking.

activities.name: Photography with Heather Wood
activities.description: A practical, hands-on session exploring how to capture natural portraits and dynamic action shots. Learn techniques on how to work well with people, use available light and surroundings, and create images full of character and personality. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: Please bring any device you can take photographs with – a smartphone is perfect, but digital cameras, film cameras and DSLRs are all welcome. This session will begin inside the craft tent, but then move around the site, so please wear appropriate footwear and clothing

activities.name: Scavenger Hunt
activities.description: Join the comedy and award-winning storytelling duo Cunning Stunts for the first-ever Camp VC Scavenger Hunt. Expect epic (and not-so-epic) prizes, team challenges, and cryptic clues. Note that this activity will include moving around the event site to complete challenges. You're welcome to sign up as an individual or join with friends, and we'll help split you into teams so nobody is flying solo!

activities.name: Pennant Flag Making
activities.description: Join this chilled class and follow the simple steps to create your own impactful penant using cardboard, felt, scissors and imagination! A perfect momento to put on your wall at home or inside your van! Perfect for beginners, but open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed.

activities.name: Intro to Skateboarding Tricks
activities.description: If you can roll around and want to try some flat ground tricks, this is the class for you. From mount flips and shuv its, to learning your first ollie. No Complete beginners. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Emergency Bicycle Repairs on Trails
activities.description: Picture it: You’re out on the trail, your bike starts having issues, but you didn’t bring an entire bike workshop with you in your backpack... This workshop will cover little fixes and temporary workarounds to get you back up and cycling whilst on trails. Discover staple kit items, common faults, and tips on how to sort them...at least enough to get you home safe! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Pet Portraits - Drop In
activities.description: Level up your home decor with a whimsical portrait, starring your pet as your muse! Bring a picture of your pet to work from, and we'll guide you to capture their likeness and accurately colour mix. You'll leave with a one-of-a-kind pet portrait in acrylic paint. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: 90s Friendship Bracelets - Drop In
activities.description: Let’s take it back to the 90’s with cute friendship bracelets! Whether you decide to make yours for a loved one, a new CAMP VC bestie, or yourself, we’ll supply you with all the funky colours, patterns and letters you need to create this perfect retro keepsake. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Plaster Casting
activities.description: Get hands-on in this playful small-scale casting workshop, where you’ll create your own sculpted finger cast to take home — as provocative or as peaceful as you like! Using simple mould-making and casting techniques, you’ll learn the process step-by-step in a relaxed, supportive setting. Come curious and leave with a tiny sculpture full of personality! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Pleasure Workshop
activities.description: Sex Ed taught a lot of us fear, shame, and what not to do. Not much about desire, joy, consent, or what we actually want. This workshop with The Pleasure Project flips the Sex Ed script, exploring what a sex-positive world could look like through playful prompts, conversation and a shared “fantasy bank” of pleasure, joy and fulfilment. There's zero pressure to share anything personal, just the aim to leave with a better way to talk about sex.

activities.name: Moto Talk: Royal Enfield Panel
activities.description: Royal Enfield believes motorcycling should be for everyone. To celebrate CAMP VC's 10th anniversary, we're joined by the Royal Enfield team for a conversation about how they're helping to reshape motorcycle culture and bring more women into the riding community. From groundbreaking programmes like Build Train Race to the barriers that still exist for new riders, we'll explore what's changing, what still needs to happen, and why the future of motorcycling depends on making the sport more inclusive. Grab a drink and join the conversation.

activities.name: Camp VC Football Games
activities.description: Fancy a solid kick about with your mates, new or existing? Come along to CAMP VC's Footie Games, either on your own or with an existing 5-a-side team, and play in a speedy match. Each match is 15 minutes, so expect fast and furious fun! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Inch Perfect - Wheelie School (£)
activities.description: This class is open to riders who already have off-road or trials experience and good bike control skills. This session is not open to absolute beginners or beginners to trails, but check out the other Inch Perfect sessions if that's you! Seasoned dirt-rider looking to progress your skills? We've got you! Get ready for some advanced tuition and higher-level skills training to master the art of the wheelie on one of our specialist trials bikes. £109 for 2 hours. Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10am on Saturday, 4th July. Please click here to be taken to the booking platform. 2) Click the third-party link above to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! Please Read: By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. You must sign the waiver you'll receive by email directly from Inch Perfect to participate. Inch Perfect provide bikes, insurance, helmets, boots, pants and jerseys in a broad range of sizes. Wear whatever you like under the kit, as long as you're comfortable (fitness gear always works well)

activities.name: Free Crafting - Drop In
activities.description: Join us for an extended, open studio session with no scheduled workshop. Your time is your own: relax and chat, get creative on something new, or continue working on any projects you started during the CAMP VC weekend. Perfect for those who love a bit of crafty parallel play! NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Permission Slips
activities.description: Stop getting in your own way. Join the fabulous Lu from LuInLuland and The Self Love Revolution to hear her set of the very best tips and tricks to permit yourself to live your biggest, boldest, most badass life.

activities.name: Acoustic Performers
activities.description: Slow down and soak up the atmosphere with live acoustic sets from a selection of talented artists.

activities.name: Moto Games
activities.description: How SLOW can you go?! If you came to CAMP VC on a bike this year, come battle it out in the legendary Camp VC Moto Games to find out and win one of the epic prizes up for grabs! If you didn't bring a bike, pull up a seat and watch the epic moto battles commence! All bikes are welcome for the MotoGames, so run whatcha brung and show us whatcha got, people! Our Best Bike Competition will follow the Moto Games madness, so stick around to find out if you're a winner! NOTE: Helmets and road-legal bikes are mandatory requirements to participate in the Camp VC Moto Games.

activities.name: Slam Poetry Workshop
activities.description: Poems get a bad rep, but this workshop will show you that actually the world of written and performed prose can be raw, DIY, uncensored, and a powerful way to take a stand. We want those who write already and want to learn more, and those who have never penned a verse but want to have a go, and even those who hate poems. Join us, be inspired, write some stuff and maybe even perform it. Let's go punks!

activities.name: Headline Dance Class: Chappell Roan
activities.description: The Dance Sissies are back, and this year, we're channelling our inner Chappell Roan with an iconic routine to Super Graphic Ultra Modern Girl. As always, this dance class is inclusive, easy to follow, and in a style that lives in the sweet spot between sexy and silly . Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. Please wear trainers. NOTE: Don't tell anyone, but there may also be a Chappell Roan flash mob moment on Saturday night’s dance floor... and you don't wanna be the only one who doesn't know those moves!

activities.name: Beaded Embroidery
activities.description: Explore how to use beads to embroider, stitch, and embellish to create unique decorative pieces. Follow the steps to learn the basics of bead embroidery techniques and create your own bead-embroidered motif. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Mandala Making
activities.description: Mandalas represent wholeness and unity and have been created cross-culturally for over 2000 years. Learn the basics to make your own mandala. With a focus on pattern development and freehand drawing, you'll create your own mandala outline to colour in and decorate. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Live Stream: British Strong Woman Competition
activities.description: The fates decided that the two epic events would happen on the very same weekend: CAMP VC in Wales and the British Strong Woman competition in York! Join Becca and the Strong Woman Coaching crew under the Red Bull tent for a live stream of this epic national event! NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the scheduled hours, and (if needed) we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis. This activity may move locations if the weather is bad, but you'll be notified beforehand.

activities.name: Get Ready & Glitter up!
activities.description: Get ready with us for the epic CAMP VC fancy dress night! Drop in to add the finishing touches to your outfit and unleash your creativity. We'll have glitter, crafting supplies, and plenty of ideas to help make your costume even more extra ! NOTE: Perfect for solo attendees Face painting available during the session for a small additional charge No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Beginner Friendly Skateboard Jam
activities.description: Join us for tunes, tricks, mini competitions, spot prizes, skateboard limbo, and more. Whether you're riding or watching, come get involved! Beginners are welcome at this jam. Prefer a jam for more advanced skaters only? We've got an Advanced Jam on Friday night. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Fire Walk (£)
activities.description: Are you ready to step beyond fear and ignite your inner strength? Join the expert team from Zen Events for an experience designed to push your limits and break through self-doubt. Fire walking barefoot across red-hot embers isn’t just about fire - it’s about facing fear head-on and realising you are stronger than you ever imagined. The firewalk is a powerful metaphor for breaking through the barriers that hold you back in life. If you can walk on fire, what else are you capable of doing? £20 per person. Paid Activity Booking Instructions 1) Click this link to book and pay for your space. Bookings open from 10am on Sat 4th July. 2) Once confirmed, click “Add to schedule” in the app to add it to your planner. Important notes: - By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. -“Add to schedule” will not secure your space, you must complete the third-party booking first. --

activities.name: Headline Moto Talk: Q+A with Leah Tokelove
activities.description: From racing motorcycles as a kid to becoming one of the UK's most celebrated flat track riders, Leah Tokelove has spent her life redefining what's possible on two wheels. She first came to CAMP VC at 17 and has been a huge part of this community, teaching tonnes of women how to ride through Days on the Dirt. Grab a drink and join us for an inspiring chat as Leah shares stories from the track, lessons in resilience and confidence, and how Days on the Dirt is helping more women discover the freedom, adventure, and joy of motorcycling.

activities.name: Saturday Roller Disco
activities.description: D-I-S-C-O! Join us for the Camp VC Roller Disco! Open to quad skaters with existing experience, or anyone who has successfully completed a Beginners Lesson and mastered the basics. Skates, helmets and pads are available to borrow on a first-come, first-served basis, although we strongly encourage you to bring your own equipment. No inline skates or first-time skaters, please. Helmets and pads are mandatory. Drop in at any point during the 90-minute session. If it's busy, participants may be asked to take turns so everyone gets a chance to skate.

activities.name: Raffle Draw
activities.description: Head to the main stage as we announce the lucky winners of the Camp VC Raffle, including the winners of the VC X Bell Custom Helmets. Grab your tickets from the Camp VC merch stand before it's too late!

activities.name: Cosmic Cabaret
activities.description: Join us for Camp VC's Cosmic Cabaret and let us transport you to the dystopian future of your dreams! Expect out-of-this-world performances and, of course, a few surprises! There will be prizes for the best-dressed dystopian beings onsite, so bring your strangest, sparkliest, most futuristic A-game, people!

activities.name: Open Mic Hour
activities.description: New to 2026, Camp VC's very first open mic has landed! Join us at the BBA tent to share a poem, story, monologue, song, diary entry... anything your heart desires! This is an open floor for you to get what you need off your chest, be heard, and celebrated. Whether it's a 30-second rant or 5 minutes of stand-up, the CAMP VC Open Mic Hour is ready for you. NOTE Please arrive 10 minutes early to sign up for a slot Slot allocations will be given on a first-come, first-served basis

activities.name: DJ Set with Girli
activities.description: Join us on the dance floor with Girli.

activities.name: Slow Flow Yoga
activities.description: This is a gentle approach to vinyasa yoga to help you move, build connections, and relax into the day ahead. Slower doesn't always mean easier, so expect some energising elements, as well as space to rest and refresh. NOTE: Open to all levels and abilities Mats are provided Wear something comfortable for movement If any yoga session is fully booked but you brought your own mat to CAMP VC, then feel free to join in!

activities.name: Zine Making
activities.description: In this hands-on, no-rules workshop, you’ll cut, collage, write, and stitch your way into the world of DIY publishing. Whether you're telling a story, starting a mini comic, or just making something weird and wonderful, this class is all about getting your ideas out of your head and onto the page — your way. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Moto Talk: Camping & Packing
activities.description: Moto camping can come with some logistical challenges! Learn how to load up safely, get some kit recommendations, pick up a packing list, and make your next moto camping trip a success.

activities.name: Nunchucks
activities.description: Learn the basics of how to handle this ancient martial arts training weapon. Practice your new skills with foam nunchucks (rather than wooden ones) so you can safely focus on technique and not lose any fingers! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. Comfortable clothing and footwear suitable for movement.

activities.name: Mountain Bike Coaching: Beginner Skills (£)
activities.description: During this beginner small group workshop, you will cover the fundamentals of mountain biking, including: Correct Body Position Vision Effective Braking Intro to Cornering Momentum Perfect for beginners, but open to all skill levels and abilities. £20 for 1.5 hours Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. 2) Click here to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! Please Read: Spaces are very limited to ensure a quality coaching experience Options to bring your own bike to the session, or arrange to use one provided - please select the right choice for you on the booking platform Wear weather- and cycling-appropriate clothing Wear full shoes suitable for mountain biking - no heels, sandals, or open-toe shoes By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider.

activities.name: Festival Apothecary
activities.description: Inspired by observations of the plants horses naturally choose to graze and interact with, this workshop explores the connection between animal instinct and traditional botanical remedies. Discover the qualities of Rose Hips for nourishment and resilience, and Dandelion for restoration and release. Explore dried plants, oils and balms, view a photographic journal, sample botanical products, and create a personalised 'promises to myself' card to take away. Animals often remind us to slow down and listen to the wisdom of our bodies and the natural world. This workshop offers an opportunity to reconnect with both. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Mindful Life Drawing
activities.description: Exhale deeply and join us for an hour of peaceful life drawing exercises, designed to help you let go of your inhibitions and find your creative flow while drawing a live model. Suitable for everyone who wants to give their creative brain a massage! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required.

activities.name: Self Defense
activities.description: Our martial arts experts will teach you how to defend and counterattack against various (fake!) weapons such as guns and knives. This is a hands-on workshop, so please be prepared to be in contact with a partner. The workshop will be delivered in a fun and non-threatening way, but please be aware that it may be triggering to be in a situation that mimics an attack. Discretion is advised when it comes to taking part.

activities.name: Finance Workshop
activities.description: Money chats that aren’t a snore. Sara Maxwell is back with a session on knowing your worth, getting real about money, and taking some of the shame, panic, and mystery out of finances. A mix of honest conversation, practical thinking, and gentle financial confidence-building for anyone who wants to feel a bit more in control.

activities.name: Mountain Bike Coaching: Intermediate Skills (£)
activities.description: This is an intermediate small-group coaching session for those who have already hit blue grade trails as a minimum, and would be fantastic for those who've started on red trails but have never had any professional coaching. Not yet? Check out the Beginner Workshop. Already nailing it? Check out the Advanced Session instead! This session focuses on skills to progress and improve your flow on the trails. Body Position Vision Effective Braking Cornering Momentum Pumping and Line Choice £20 for 1.5 hours Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. 2) Click here to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! Please Read: Spaces are very limited to ensure a quality coaching experience Options to bring your own bike to the session, or arrange to use one provided - please select the right choice for you on the booking platform Wear weather- and cycling-appropriate clothing Wear full shoes suitable for mountain biking - no heels, sandals, or open-toe shoes By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. PLEASE ARRIVE AT THE MEETING POINT AT LEAST 10 MINUTES BEFORE YOUR SESSION STARTS

activities.name: Words that Move
activities.description: A creative, immersive writing and art workshop where your expression and voice are transformed into visual form. Use the quick prompts to uncover your thoughts and truths, then transform these into bold, visual art through typography, collage and mark-making. Leave with a one-off artwork made from your own words that you can keep, gift, or swap with someone else! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name: Beaded Embroidery - Drop In
activities.description: Explore how to use beads to embroider, stitch, and embellish to create unique decorative pieces. Follow the steps to learn the basics of bead embroidery techniques and create your own bead-embroidered motif. Open to all levels and abilities - no experience required. NOTE: No booking is required for drop-in activities. Come along during the opening hours, and we'll allocate spaces on a first-come, first-served basis.

activities.name:
Intro to Mini Ramps (Skateboarding) 
- longer session
activities.description: Learn the basics of the mini ramp: pumping, turning and maybe even dropping in! You don’t need to be a seasoned skater for this one, but you must be able to roll comfortably on a skateboard already. No complete beginners. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed.

activities.name: Camp VC Football Tournament
activities.description: The epic Camp VC Mini Football Tournament is BACK! Come along as a fully formed 5-aside team, or come solo / as a smaller group and join a new team on the day! Tournament teams will be 5-aside with rolling subs. Tournament prizes will include: Best Goal Celebration, Friendliest Player, The Camp VC keepy-uppy Champion, and others to be announced! Not to be missed and great for solo attendees! Open to all levels and abilities - no experience needed.

activities.name: QueerSwap: Visible Mending and Embroidery Workshop
activities.description: Settle in for a cozy workshop learning how to save your clothing by creating or applying a patch, fixing a busted seam, or embroidering beautiful designs over stains and holes. All supplies are provided.

activities.name: ADHD AF Live Podcast
activities.description: Laura Mears-Reynolds was diagnosed with ADHD at 38 in 2022, and she’s not shut up about it ever since. She has recorded over 100 podcast episodes, covering heartbreak, relationship tips, new techniques to manage RSD, how to spot a failing relationship before it's too late and SO MUCH MORE! Come join us for a Camp VC live special where she'll be talking to Lu Featherstone about the weekend and what the Camp VC community has taught them

activities.name: Open Ramp Skateboarding
activities.description: Come and join Shankos Skate School to hang out and ride the ramp. This is an open skate session with no coaching provided, so you’ll need previous skateboarding experience and be confident riding independently. We recommend bringing your own board, pads, and helmet for this session, but kit can be borrowed if needed. No complete beginners. Please wear suitable trainers. No sandals, crocs, heels or platform shoes.

activities.name: Mountain Bike Coaching: Advanced Skills (£)
activities.description: This is an advanced small-group coaching session for those who have hit red grade trails as a minimum. Ideal for those ready to progress who've never had any professional coaching. Not hit red trails just yet? Check out the Beginner or Intermediate Workshops instead. You'll learn advanced skills to progress and improve your flow on the trails, including: Cornering Correct Body Position Effective Braking Line Choice Intro to jumps and drops £20 for 1.5 hours Paid Activity Booking Instructions: 1) Bookings open from 10 am on Saturday, 4th July. 2) Click here to book and pay for your space. 3) Once booked and paid, click “Add to schedule” to add it to your planner. Important! Please Read: Spaces are very limited to ensure a quality coaching experience Options to bring your own bike and helmet to the session, or arrange to use one provided - please select the right choice for you on the booking platform Wear weather- and cycling-appropriate clothing Wear full shoes suitable for mountain biking - no heels, sandals, or open-toe shoes By booking with a third party, you agree to their terms and conditions, including refund and cancellation policies. Clicking “Add to schedule” will not secure your space; you must complete the third-party booking first. Any queries regarding paid activities should be directed to the activity provider. PLEASE ARRIVE AT THE MEETING POINT AT LEAST 10 MINUTES BEFORE YOUR SESSION STARTS

activities.name: Here and Queer
activities.description: Join us for a session that is part talk, part workshop, part mediation, and part something else entirely! Yes, just like most of us, it's a lot more than one thing. Take a deeper look into LGBTQIA+ identities in a safe and welcoming space as we encourage you to explore, express, and accept yourself a little more, wherever you may be on your journey.
